name: Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals Ltd
oneLine: Cert expires 2026-05-05 (8 days from scan); missing CSP and Referrer-Policy

tls.note: IMMINENT EXPIRY — outage risk if not rotated

findings: TLS cert expires 2026-05-05 — 7 days remaining at this writing
Email auth strong (DMARC p=quarantine, SPF strict)
Site connectivity issues at scan time

urgentActions.what: Rotate TLS certificate before May 5 to avoid outage

fingerprints.stack: Apache (Server returned 503 at scan time — load balancer or maintenance)
versions: GlobalSign RSA OV SSL CA 2018 TLS — EXPIRES 2026-05-05 (7 DAYS — IMMINENT)
eolFlags: CERT EXPIRY in 7 days — Tier 1 incident; missing CSP; site connectivity issues at scan time

attackPaths.name: Cert-expiry MITM window (May 5–25)
attackPaths.entry: After May 5, browsers reject the cert. Attacker acquires fake wildcard via Let's Encrypt; intercepts traffic until real cert pinned again.
attackPaths.pivot: Refinery staff receive fake SAP ERP login page; admin credentials harvested.
attackPaths.objective: MITM credential harvest at refinery scale.

attackPaths.name: SAP.MRPL subdomain hijack
attackPaths.entry: Exploit exposed credentials or plugin RCE on sap.mrpl.co.in.
attackPaths.pivot: Lateral move to financial / production systems.
attackPaths.objective: Operational security impact; production data exfil.

attackPaths.name: Weak cert renewal chain → MITM persists
attackPaths.entry: If renewed with weaker CA or missing pinning.
attackPaths.pivot: MITM via cross-signed deprecated CA; malware distribution masked as official updates.

attackPaths.name: ERP procurement compromise
attackPaths.entry: ERP exposure surfaces procurement / vendor payment data.
attackPaths.pivot: Phishing supplier mailboxes with invoice forgery; refinery payment diverted to attacker accounts.

paths.preAi: Cert-expiry MITM is opportunistic; success window narrow
paths.mythos: Pre-staged Let's Encrypt + automated DNS hijack reduces window from days to minutes
Subdomain takeover combined with SAP CVE chain compresses dwell from weeks to days

cisoPatchList.title: EMERGENCY: Rotate TLS certificate before 2026-05-05
cisoPatchList.fix: Order new cert IMMEDIATELY (target 2026-04-29 install). OV cert from established CA. CSR strength: SHA-256 RSA 2048+ minimum. Install on all SANs (mrpl.co.in, *.mrpl.co.in, *.sap.mrpl.co.in).

cisoPatchList.title: Verify cert deploys to all subdomains (sap, www, etc.)
cisoPatchList.fix: Deploy cert to load balancer + all reverse proxy endpoints.
cisoPatchList.validation: curl -sI https://sap.mrpl.co.in returns 200 with valid cert

cisoPatchList.title: Implement HPKP (HTTP Public Key Pinning) for backup cert key
cisoPatchList.fix: Pin current cert + backup CA root. 30-day max-age. Phased rollout to avoid lockout.

cisoPatchList.title: Audit and disable unused subdomains; document SANs
cisoPatchList.fix: nslookup *.mrpl.co.in. Decommission unused. Document each in SAN list.

cisoPatchList.title: Certificate pinning on client applications (mobile / API)
cisoPatchList.fix: Pin backup cert public key; phased SDK rollout.
cisoPatchList.validation: Client SDK logs show pin validation on TLS handshake

phase2.headlineQuestion: Can MRPL rotate the expiring certificate (7 days to expiry) and implement HPKP before the May 5–25 window opens for MITM credential harvesting at the refinery?
